key_insight: CatRetriever learns a shared latent space for slabs and bulk crystals so a generated catalyst surface can be mapped back to plausible parent bulk materials.
engineering_relevance: This enables catalyst discovery workflows to assess bulk-dependent stability and synthesizability, not just surface activity, making generative design more physically useful.
method: Contrastive representation learning aligns slab and bulk crystal embeddings.
A slab query retrieves candidate parent bulks by nearest-neighbor search in latent space.
An extended pipeline combines retrieval with generative search and adsorption-energy distribution screening.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong retrieval performance, with R@1 above 91% and R@3 above 98% on both in-distribution and holdout test sets.
watch_item: Retrieval quality may depend on the training database coverage, so novel chemistries or poorly represented surface motifs could still be missed.
paper_title: CatRetriever: Contrastive Representation Learning for Slab-to-Bulk Retrieval in Generative Catalyst Discovery
